briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for June 11, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Urgent Climate Crisis Warning and El Niño's Onset and Expansion
sections.content: Recently, winter temperatures in Antarctica reached 15.4℃, leading scientists to warn that the pace of climate collapse is faster than anticipated [4]. According to various climate reports, the 1.5°C global warming limit is highly likely to be exceeded by 2030, or even within four years [46, 50, 61, 63]. Furthermore, research has revealed that certain overlooked pollutants are currently contributing to approximately 15% of global warming [40, 55]. In addition, the U.S. National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) has officially announced the onset of El Niño, with experts warning that this will accelerate extreme weather events [42, 66, 69, 71]. These changes are causing severe ecological and socioeconomic impacts worldwide, including damage to northern peatlands, deaths of rare orangutans, and the drying up of lakes in Hungary [51, 57, 58, 67].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Carbon Neutrality Practices and Policy Trends
sections.content: Domestically, active movements towards achieving carbon neutrality goals have been observed. Korea has strengthened international cooperation by holding the 1st Joint Committee on Climate Change Cooperation with Peru and Kyrgyzstan, respectively, to discuss greenhouse gas reduction projects [1, 3]. Local governments and institutions are encouraging citizen participation and capacity building through carbon neutrality expert training, environmental education week operations, and carbon neutrality point incentives [8, 11, 12, 13, 18, 20, 23, 24, 25, 26]. Seoul Energy Corporation is practicing carbon neutrality through a bicycle commuting campaign, and the Korean Intellectual Property Office visited Hyundai Steel to gather on-site opinions for strengthening carbon neutrality and future steel competitiveness, demonstrating ongoing transition efforts in the industrial sector [9, 10, 19]. Furthermore, news of resource security and carbon neutrality practices through the recycling of discarded telecommunication equipment, along with the resumption of the review of the 'Framework Act on Carbon Neutrality,' raises expectations for strengthening the domestic policy foundation [21, 27].

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Renewable Energy and Investment in Eco-friendly Technologies
sections.content: Globally, investment in renewable energy transition and eco-friendly technologies continues to expand. The European Union (EU) has approved Italy's $26 billion renewable energy plan, which will add 37 GW of clean power [29], and in Indonesia, the Energy Agency has launched an agri-photovoltaic project [31]. Domestically, H-Energy is supporting the carbon-neutral transition of small and medium-sized enterprises by supplying RE100 power based on solar and energy storage systems (ESS) to the Anseong Industrial Complex [30, 33]. Furthermore, Goldman Sachs has presented a positive outlook for the U.S. renewable energy market, including upgrading the buy ratings for renewable energy companies, and the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) is promoting bio-inputs to combat climate change [32, 34, 53].

sections.title: ⚠️ Widespread Impacts of Climate Change and Policy Challenges
sections.content: Climate change extends beyond a mere environmental issue, exerting severe impacts across economic and social systems. Analysis suggests that the assets of the ultra-wealthy are exacerbating climate crisis debt, leading to an estimated $1 trillion in annual climate damages, and concerns are growing that climate change is making our lives more expensive [6, 37]. News of the federal government's planned dismantling of climate change and ocean monitoring systems has raised warnings that it could disrupt critical data collection [35]. Furthermore, questions have been raised about whether the transition to renewable energy should be left solely to the market, alongside arguments that discussions on climate change cannot be ignored due to high energy prices [28, 36]. In this context, the Daejeon-Chungnam Green Union has urged Mayor-elect Huh Tae-jung to comprehensively revise existing energy and carbon neutrality plans, underscoring the urgent need for policy reconsideration and innovation to address the climate crisis [15].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted both the urgency of the climate crisis and the diverse domestic and international efforts to overcome it. Through continued attention and cooperation, we must seek effective solutions to climate change. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: ‘Super El Niño’ is officially here, scientists say. What can we expect?
articles.description: Experts say climate pattern could supercharge extreme weather events and push temperatures to record highsEL Niño has officially arrived, US officials at the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (Noaa) said on Thursday, and scientists predict it could be the strongest of the century.Forecasters had previously anticipated that a phenomenon known as a super “El Niño” would emerge this summer – supercharging extreme weather events and pushing global temperatures to record heights. Contin

summary.phenomenon: Officials at the U.S. National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) announced on Thursday that El Niño has officially arrived, and scientists predict it could be the strongest of the century.
summary.outlook: This climate pattern could supercharge extreme weather events and push global temperatures to record highs.

articles.original_description: Experts say climate pattern could supercharge extreme weather events and push temperatures to record highsEL Niño has officially arrived, US officials at the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (Noaa) said on Thursday, and scientists predict it could be the strongest of the century.Forecasters had previously anticipated that a phenomenon known as a super “El Niño” would emerge this summer – supercharging extreme weather events and pushing global temperatures to record heights. articles.title: Subterranean fungi networks more than 100 quadrillion km in length, study finds
articles.description: First ever global mapping of arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi shows scale of hyphal systems that sustain plant lifeOur planet’s soils contain enough of the subterranean fungi that sustain plant life and help regulate the climate to stretch from the Earth to the sun almost three-quarters of a billion times, a groundbreaking new study has found.Arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi are networks of tubular cells called hyphae that sustain life on Earth by forming critical partnerships with more than 70% of plan

summary.phenomenon: A new study has created the first global mapping of arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi, revealing that the subterranean fungal network spans over 1 quintillion kilometers.
summary.cause: Arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi are networks of hyphae, tubular cells that sustain life on Earth by forming critical partnerships with over 70% of plants.
summary.outlook: These fungi help sustain plant life on Earth and regulate the climate.

articles.title: 태평양에 엘니뇨 형성, 전문가들 극한 기상 가속화할 것으로 전망
articles.original_title: El Niño forms in Pacific as experts say it will likely turbocharge extreme weather
articles.description: Meteorologists predict it will be the strongest of century, while UN secretary-general calls it ‘urgent climate warning’El Niño, the climate phenomenon that supercharges weather around the world, has officially arrived and could intensify to historic levels in the fall, US officials said on Thursday.US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (Noaa) forecasters confirmed the formation of El Niño in the warmer than usual Pacific Ocean near the equator, which affects global weather patterns

summary.phenomenon: US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (Noaa) forecasters confirmed on Thursday the official formation of the El Niño phenomenon in the warmer than usual Pacific Ocean near the equator.
summary.cause: El Niño is a climate phenomenon that supercharges weather around the world, forming in the warmer than usual Pacific Ocean near the equator and affecting global weather patterns.
summary.outlook: El Niño could intensify to historic levels this fall, with meteorologists predicting it will be the strongest of the century. The UN Secretary-General has referred to it as an 'urgent climate warning'.
